title: Pattern matching tool
abstract: The present disclosure is directed to a software tool that engages in a pattern matching technique. In one implementation, the software tool retrieves a two-dimensional drawing and identifies walls as lines, rotates the drawing until a threshold number of lines are aligned with either the X or Y axes, discards lines that are not aligned with either the X or Y axis, identifies intersection points, identifies a subset of intersection points that have a maxima or minima coordinate, constructs a data library indicative of the relative positions of the points in the identified subset; and compares the constructed data libraries for the two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for another two-dimensional drawing.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1. A computing system comprising: at least one processor; a non-transitory computer-readable medium; and program instructions stored on the non-transitory computer-readable medium that are executable by the at least one processor to cause the computing system to: retrieve a first two-dimensional drawing representative of a portion of a construction project; identify lines on the two-dimensional drawing and points of intersection between two or more identified lines; from among the identified points of intersection between two or more lines, identify a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two- dimensional drawing; construct a respective data library for each respective point of intersection in the identified subset, the respective data library comprising respective data mappings describing the relative orientation and distance to the respective point of intersection from each other point of intersection in the identified subset; and compare the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing to thereby determine whether the first two-dimensional drawing and the second two-dimensional drawing depict the same portion of the construction project. 2. The computing system of claim 1, wherein the computing system further comprises program instructions that are executable by the at least one processor to cause the computing system to: identify lines on the two-dimensional drawing that are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the first two-dimensional drawing; discard from the two-dimensional drawing lines that were identified as not being aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing such that remaining lines on the first two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing; and 

 identify, from among the remaining lines, points of intersection between two or more remaining lines. 3. The computing system of claim 1, wherein the computing system further comprises program instructions that are executable by the at least one processor to cause the computing system to: determine that at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; and in response to the determining, rotate the first two-dimensional drawing until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing. 4. The computing system of claim 1, wherein the computing system further comprises program instructions that are executable by the at least one processor to cause the computing system to: determine that at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; and in response to the determining, (i) rotate the first two-dimensional drawing clockwise until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing, and (ii) rotate the first two-dimensional drawing counter-clockwise until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the counter-clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; determine which of the (a) clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing, and (b) the counter-clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing were rotated to a lesser extent, and use that determined rotated two-dimensional drawing to (1) identify a subset of the points of intersection, (2) construct a respective data library, and (3) compare the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection. 5. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the program instructions executable to cause the computing system to identify a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two-dimensional drawing comprise program instructions that are executable by the at least one processor to cause the computing system to: assign x-y coordinates to each identified point of intersection; determine, from among the assigned x-y coordinates, a maximum x-coordinate, a maximum y-coordinate, a minimum x-coordinate, and a minimum y-coordinate; and identify, as the subset of points of intersection that have either a maximum or minimum coordinate, intersection points that have assigned coordinates containing at least one of the maximum x-coordinate, the maximum y-coordinate, the minimum x-coordinate, and the minimum y-coordinate. 6. The computing device of claim 1, wherein the program instructions executable to cause the computing system to identify a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two-dimensional drawing comprise program instructions that are executable by the at least one processor to cause the computing system to: for each respective identified point of intersection, generate a Cartesian plane with the origin located at the respective identified point of intersection, the Cartesian plane being divided into four quadrants; for each respective quadrant in each respective Cartesian plane, determine whether any other identified points of intersection fall within the respective quadrant; and identify, as the subset of points of intersection that have either a maximum or minimum coordinate, intersection points that have at least one respective Cartesian plane quadrant having no other identified points of intersection located therein. 7. The computing system of claim 1, wherein the program instructions executable to cause the computing system to compare the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing comprise program instructions that are executable by the at least one processor to cause the computing system to: 

 determine a scale factor based on a first comparison between the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing; and apply the determined scale factor to distance components of the data mappings of the data library for the first two-dimensional drawing; and engage in a second comparison between a scale-factor-adjusted data library for the first two-dimensional drawing, and the data library for the second two-dimensional drawing to thereby determine whether the first two-dimensional drawing and the second two-dimensional drawing depict the same portion of the construction project. 8. A method comprising: retrieving a first two-dimensional drawing representative of a portion of a construction project; identifying lines on the two-dimensional drawing and points of intersection between two or more identified lines; from among the identified points of intersection between two or more lines, identifying a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two-dimensional drawing; constructing a respective data library for each respective point of intersection in the identified subset, the respective data library comprising respective data mappings describing the relative orientation and distance to the respective point of intersection from each other point of intersection in the identified subset; and comparing the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing to thereby determine whether the first two-dimensional drawing and the second two- dimensional drawing depict the same portion of the construction project. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising: identifying lines on the two-dimensional drawing that are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the first two-dimensional drawing; discarding from the two-dimensional drawing lines that were identified as not being aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing such that remaining lines on the first two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing; and 

 identifying, from among the remaining lines, points of intersection between two or more remaining lines. 10. The method of claim 8, further comprising: determining that at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; and in response to the determining, rotating the first two-dimensional drawing until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing. 11. The method of claim 8, further comprising: determining that at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; and in response to the determining, (i) rotating the first two-dimensional drawing clockwise until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing, and (ii) rotating the first two-dimensional drawing counter-clockwise until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the counter-clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; determining which of the (a) clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing, and (b) the counter-clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing were rotated to a lesser extent, and using that determined rotated two-dimensional drawing to (1) identify a subset of the points of intersection, (2) construct a respective data library, and (3) compare the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein identifying a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two-dimensional drawing comprises: assigning x-y coordinates to each identified point of intersection; determining, from among the assigned x-y coordinates, a maximum x-coordinate, a maximum y-coordinate, a minimum x-coordinate, and a minimum y-coordinate; and 

 identifying, as the subset of points of intersection that have either a maximum or minimum coordinate, intersection points that have assigned coordinates containing at least one of the maximum x-coordinate, the maximum y-coordinate, the minimum x-coordinate, and the minimum y-coordinate. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein identifying a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two-dimensional drawing comprises: for each respective identified point of intersection, generating a Cartesian plane with the origin located at the respective identified point of intersection, the Cartesian plane being divided into four quadrants; for each respective quadrant in each respective Cartesian plane, determining whether any other identified points of intersection fall within the respective quadrant; and identifying, as the subset of points of intersection that have either a maximum or minimum coordinate, intersection points that have at least one respective Cartesian plane quadrant having no other identified points of intersection located therein. 14. The method of claim 8, wherein comparing the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing comprises: determining a scale factor based on a first comparison between the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing; and applying the determined scale factor to distance components of the data mappings of the data library for the first two-dimensional drawing; and engaging in a second comparison between a scale-factor-adjusted data library for the first two-dimensional drawing, and the data library for the second two-dimensional drawing to thereby determine whether the first two-dimensional drawing and the second two-dimensional drawing depict the same portion of the construction project. 15. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium having program instructions stored thereon that are executable to cause a computing system to: retrieve a first two-dimensional drawing representative of a portion of a construction project; 

 identify lines on the two-dimensional drawing and points of intersection between two or more identified lines; from among the identified points of intersection between two or more lines, identify a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two-dimensional drawing; construct a respective data library for each respective point of intersection in the identified subset, the respective data library comprising respective data mappings describing the relative orientation and distance to the respective point of intersection from each other point of intersection in the identified subset; and compare the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing to thereby determine whether the first two-dimensional drawing and the second two- dimensional drawing depict the same portion of the construction project. 16. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the program instructions are further executable to cause the computing system to: identify lines on the two-dimensional drawing that are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the first two-dimensional drawing; discard from the two-dimensional drawing lines that were identified as not being aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing such that remaining lines on the first two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing; and identify, from among the remaining lines, points of intersection between two or more remaining lines. 17. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the program instructions are further executable to cause the computing system to: determine that at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; and in response to the determining, rotate the first two-dimensional drawing until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two-dimensional drawing. 18. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the program instructions are further executable to cause the computing system to: determine that at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the two-dimensional drawing are not aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; and in response to the determining, (i) rotate the first two-dimensional drawing clockwise until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing, and (ii) rotate the first two-dimensional drawing counter-clockwise until at least a threshold amount of identified lines on the counter-clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing are aligned to at least a threshold extent with either an x-axis or a y-axis of the two- dimensional drawing; determine which of the (a) clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing, and (b) the counter-clockwise-rotated two-dimensional drawing were rotated to a lesser extent, and use that determined rotated two-dimensional drawing to (1) identify a subset of the points of intersection, (2) construct a respective data library, and (3) compare the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the program instructions executable to cause the computing system to identify a subset of the points of intersection in which each point of intersection in the identified subset has either a maximum or minimum coordinate within the first two-dimensional drawing comprise program instructions that are executable to cause the computing system to: for each respective identified point of intersection, generate a Cartesian plane with the origin located at the respective identified point of intersection, the Cartesian plane being divided into four quadrants; for each respective quadrant in each respective Cartesian plane, determine whether any other identified points of intersection fall within the respective quadrant; and identify, as the subset of points of intersection that have either a maximum or minimum coordinate, intersection points that have at least one respective Cartesian plane quadrant having no other identified points of intersection located therein. 20. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the program instructions executable to cause the computing system to compare the data libraries 

 constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing comprise program instructions that are executable to cause the computing system to: determine a scale factor based on a first comparison between the data libraries constructed for the identified subset of points of intersection for the first two-dimensional drawing to data libraries constructed for a second two-dimensional drawing; and apply the determined scale factor to distance components of the data mappings of the data library for the first two-dimensional drawing; and engage in a second comparison between a scale-factor-adjusted data library for the first two-dimensional drawing, and the data library for the second two-dimensional drawing to thereby determine whether the first two-dimensional drawing and the second two-dimensional drawing depict the same portion of the construction project.
